title: extract_match_type/comparator/address_part: filter-with-mutation is subtle
description: All three extract_* functions use .filter() with a closure that mutates a captured local variable (mt/cmp/part) as a side effect, using the filter's return value to strip matched tokens. This pattern is non-obvious: it works because iterators are sequential and Rust ensures exclusive capture, but a reader unfamiliar with the trick will re-read it twice. Suggested rewrite: a simple for loop that builds a Vec<&Form> of remaining forms and records the tag, making the mutation explicit.
